title: dalbergia lemurica
intro: Dalbergia lemurica, also known as Madagascar rosewood, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia lemurica include its potential to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the bark of the tree is used in herbal preparations, often being dried and powdered to create a medicinal paste or infused in hot water to create a tea.
